title: Festuca calva
taxonomy: Festuca calva is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is classified as a member of the genus Festuca, which is a large and diverse group of grasses. Festuca calva is further classified as belonging to the subgenus Festuca, which includes many species of grasses found in temperate regions. The species is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. Its classification is based on a combination of morphological and molecular characteristics.
uses: Festuca calva is native to certain regions and is known to have various uses, but specifically, there is limited information available on its medicinal uses. Festuca calva has not been widely studied or documented for its medicinal properties. However, its close relative, Festuca rubra, has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The exact uses of Festuca calva in medicine are unclear. More research is needed to determine the potential medicinal uses of this plant species.
